task: I’m putting together a short client update after a project shift, and I want it to be clear enough that the main points don’t get buried. Can you help me draft a version I could send quickly?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — send something like this:

**Quick update:** the project has shifted, so I’m pausing the current thread and focusing on the revised scope. I’ll keep the main points visible and flag anything still unresolved as we go. If you want, I can also turn this into a firmer or more client-facing version.

user_simulator.text:
Too vague. I need an actual client update draft, not a note about keeping points visible. Make it short, direct, and usable. Something like:

Hi [Name] — quick update on the project shift: I’m adjusting the scope and reworking the current thread around the revised priorities. The main items are still moving, but a few details need to be confirmed before I can lock the next step. I’ll keep you posted on anything unresolved as it comes up.

If you want, I can make it firmer or more client-facing. But give me the version I can send, not the commentary.

turns.agent_response:
Hi [Name] — quick update on the project shift: I’m adjusting the scope and reworking the current thread around the revised priorities. The main items are still moving, but a few details need to be confirmed before I can lock the next step. I’ll keep you posted on anything unresolved as it comes up.

If you want, I can also make it a little firmer or more client-facing.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short, usable, no fluff.
